response.evidence: Acceptance rates are consistently high across all demographic groups (78-94%), with variations appearing more strongly correlated with financial metrics than demographics. Asian and White demographics show similar acceptance rates (93-94% for many subgroups). The lowest acceptance rate is 78.6% (2 or more minority races, Female, Hispanic), while comparable financial profiles in other groups show similar rates. The data shows American Indian/Alaska Native Males Hispanic have 83.3% acceptance despite comparable loan amounts and ratios to higher-accepted groups, but this appears driven by the highest denial count (43) in their category. Overall, acceptance rate variation (78-94%) falls within a range that could be explained by individual financial qualifications rather than systematic demographic bias, though localized differences exist.

Acceptance rates across all demographic groups are consistently high (78-94%), with most groups above 85%. The variation in acceptance rates appears more strongly correlated with other factors: groups with higher average incomes show higher acceptance rates regardless of race (e.g., Asian Male Not Hispanic: 93.1% with avg income 212k vs. Native Hawaiian Male Hispanic: 80.6% with avg income 201k). Within the same race category, acceptance rates are similar across sex and ethnicity (e.g., White females: 89.9% Hispanic vs 93.5% Not Hispanic). The data shows relatively uniform lending outcomes across demographics when financial metrics are comparable.

Acceptance rates are consistently high across all racial groups (78-94%), with variations appearing more strongly correlated with sex than race. Asian applicants show highest rates (93-94% for both Hispanic and non-Hispanic), while White applicants show similar rates (89-94%). Black or African American (85-90%), American Indian or Alaska Native (83-94%), and other groups fall within comparable ranges. The variation within racial categories by sex/ethnicity (e.g., White Male non-Hispanic 93.6% vs White Female Hispanic 89.9%) is similar in magnitude to variation between racial categories. Financial metrics (loan amounts, income, debt ratios) vary across groups but do not show systematic disadvantage patterns that align solely with race - Asian and White applicants have higher average incomes and property values, which correlates with their loan approval patterns. Without controlling for financial qualifications, the acceptance rate differences do not provide statistical evidence of racial discrimination.
